title: All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services

narrative.executive_view: 541990 is not a coherent acquisition vertical; it is a residual code spanning appraisals, marine surveying, commodity and infrastructure inspection, weather forecasting, patent brokerage, and other specialist services. The investable unit is therefore a particular credentialed inspection/appraisal operator, not the code average. AI can compress report drafting and analytical production, but terminal demand differs sharply between a liability-bearing field assessment and an output-only forecast or report. [S1][S2][S3]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI most directly changes report drafting, desk research, photo and document triage, proposal preparation, client updates, and administrative routing. It can also pressure weather-information products: DeepMind reports that GraphCast produces 10-day forecasts in under a minute and outperformed the referenced deterministic system on most tested variables. That is evidence of capability pressure, not evidence that an inspection, appraisal, or advisory client will abandon accountable human work. [S4]
narrative.value_capture: A specialist firm may retain some savings where a referral relationship, physical inspection, signature, or liability allocation matters. It is less likely to retain savings where the buyer pays for a generic written answer that AI can cheaply generate. The Appraisal Foundation says USPAP applies across disciplines but whether it is required for non-real-property work depends on law, policy, contract, or membership; this prevents treating a broad credential story as a universal moat. [S2][S3]
narrative.adoption_timing: Effective adoption is estimated at four years because the technology is already credible in analytical work, but the code's heterogeneous workflows, client acceptance requirements, data liability, and field component slow conversion of the entire operator workflow. Current effective adoption is below 50% at the code level because no representative 541990 adoption survey was found. This is a deliberately low-confidence bridge rather than a claim of measured industry penetration. [S1][S4]
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic target count is larger than veterinary's but it is a margin-based screen across an incoherent code. There is no verified code-wide consolidator market or uniform multiple. Reopened BizBuySell data show 2025 national service-business sold earnings multiples of 2.58x on an owner's-discretionary-earnings basis, with a 2.70x 2025 average; this supports a conservative small-business proxy only, not an EBITDA transaction comp or a roll-up premium. [S5][S6]
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is pressured rather than melting for the selected inspection/appraisal cluster. Field observation, local-condition judgment, insurer/lender/client requirements, and accountable signature work can remain paid, but personal-property credentials are often voluntary and AI can commoditize the reporting layer. Output-only weather or desktop-analysis subsegments face a much more severe terminal risk, so diligence must confirm revenue mix before acquisition. [S2][S3][S4]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The two major deterministic warnings are a supplied labor-share anomaly and total role-mix contamination: the 4-digit occupation mix is veterinary-dominated and cannot describe this residual code. The finalizer must retain those inputs, but this packet makes the caveat explicit and keeps the automation estimate conservative. Key-person risk is also acute because credentials, client trust, and field experience may sit with the seller rather than the entity. [S1][S3]

sources.title: 2022 NAICS 541990 All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
sources.what_it_supports: Official code definition and illustrative activities including non-real-estate appraisal, marine survey, commodity and visual inspection, patent brokerage, arbitration, and weather forecasting.

sources.title: The Appraisal Foundation: USPAP
sources.what_it_supports: USPAP's cross-discipline scope, its guidance on AI tools, and the fact that non-real-property applicability can depend on law, client policy, contract, or membership.

sources.title: The Appraisal Foundation: Personal Property Appraisal
sources.what_it_supports: Personal-property experience and education recommendations, professional designations, and the explicit statement that a credential is not legally required to practice personal-property appraisal.

sources.title: Google DeepMind: GraphCast AI model for faster and more accurate global weather forecasting
sources.what_it_supports: AI weather-forecasting capability: under-one-minute 10-day forecasts and the author's reported comparison with HRES; it is technology evidence, not a revenue-share study for 541990.

sources.title: BizBuySell Service Business Valuation Multiples & Financial Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: National service-business sold earnings-multiple data: 2.58x average for 2021-25, 2.70x average for 2025, defined as seller's discretionary earnings/cash flow rather than EBITDA.

sources.title: BizBuySell 2025 Fourth Quarter Insight Report
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 service-sector transaction context and a reported 2.52 average cash-flow multiple; it is a broad market benchmark, not a 541990 comp set.

sources.title: AP: Starting a small business is hard. Exiting can be even harder
sources.what_it_supports: Census-attributed general small-business succession context: about 51% of owners are over 55; this is not a NAICS 541990 owner-age measure.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: The deterministic 4-digit role mix is expressly contaminated by veterinary occupations, so it cannot be read as this code's work composition. Use only a conservative task estimate for the hypothesized inspection/appraisal cluster: AI is strong in drafting, research, client administration, and analytical output but weak in field observation, accountability, and relationship work.
caveats: Every supplied SOC must be mechanically judged, but the veterinary-dominated 4-digit mix is not a valid occupation proxy for 541990.
The RESIDUAL share is 44.82% and dominates the result; target-level job mapping is mandatory before underwriting.
GraphCast demonstrates weather-forecasting capability but does not establish replaceability in appraisal or inspection firms.

role_judgments.rationale: Contaminated veterinary role: only documentation and administrative support are treated as automatable, not hands-on work.
Contaminated veterinary assistant role: the physical core is not used as evidence for 541990 automation.
Contaminated veterinarian role: treat only generic documentation work as automatable.
Scheduling, intake, routing, and routine communication are broadly automatable across specialist service firms.
Contaminated animal-care role: retain a minimal generic documentation fraction only.
Routine customer updates, status requests, and document delivery are highly automatable with escalation.
Scheduling, dispatch, reporting, and finance preparation automate; client escalation and judgment remain.
Image organization and first-pass enhancement can automate, but evidence capture and quality accountability remain.
Proposal drafts and CRM work automate; referral and trust-based selling remain.
Draft translation and document preparation are exposed, while sensitive and high-liability interpretation retains review.
Use a deliberately low residual fraction because the unobserved dominant work may be field inspection, appraisal judgment, or output-only analysis with opposite exposure profiles.

pi_dist.rationale: Use half retention: some inspection and appraisal work has relationship/accountability value, while generic report production faces competition and AI-enabled pass-through.
caveats: No code-wide pricing or margin study was reopened; this is a heterogeneous-segment judgment.

pi_moat.rationale: Standards, experience, designations, and client requirements create a partial moat for selected work, but personal-property credentials are not universally legally required and work may follow an individual professional.
caveats: Do not infer a universal legal credential barrier from USPAP or professional-organization requirements.

t50_years.rationale: Advanced capability is present in an included output-only subsegment, but code-wide effective adoption is slowed by heterogeneity, field work, liability, and client acceptance.
caveats: No representative 541990 adoption survey was found; weather-model evidence is not adoption evidence for all subsegments.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Set a low, explicitly unmeasured code-level effective-adoption starting point because the code contains both highly exposed analytical work and low-substitution field work.
caveats: This is not an observed industry adoption statistic.

historical_analogs.value: Specialist-service digitization has historically automated research, formatting, and distribution before removing accountable field judgment; weather modeling is a cautionary counterexample where the analytic output itself can be commoditized.
historical_analogs.rationale: This qualitative analogy separates professional accountability from automated output rather than asserting a measured historical curve.
caveats: No uniform historical analogue applies to every activity in this catch-all NAICS code.

selection.rationale: Translate the reopened general statistic that about 51% of small-business owners are over 55 into a deliberately lower, unverified over-60 screening assumption.
caveats: This is a US small-business age proxy, not a NAICS 541990 measurement.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: General retirement context alone does not document a code-specific shortage of successors, so do not award a documented-shortage claim.
caveats: A target may still have a genuine succession issue, but it must be confirmed in diligence.

active_consolidators.rationale: Use one as a minimal indication that specialist-service buyers exist, while refusing to claim a coherent 541990 consolidator set from broad service-market evidence.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: 0-3 meaningful buyers in a chosen subsegment
caveats: There is no verified code-wide consolidator count; this input must be replaced after selecting a precise subsegment.

buy_mult.rationale: Use the reopened 2025 national service-business average earnings multiple as a conservative purchase proxy with no subsegment premium.
caveats: The source is national service-business data and reports seller's discretionary earnings/cash flow, not 541990 adjusted EBITDA.

exit_mult.rationale: Assume no multiple expansion and retain the same broad service-business proxy at exit.
caveats: This is not an exit-market forecast and deliberately excludes a platform-multiple thesis.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed fixed-fee-per-report, inspection, appraisal, project, and subscription pricing by subsegment
pricing_structure.rationale: The official examples span appraisals, inspections, brokers, and weather services, each of which can use a different price model.
caveats: No code-wide pricing mix is available; capture cannot be underwritten until a subsegment and target are specified.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The modest, uncertain automation share is paired with a four-year adoption estimate because the potential varies radically across field, credentialed, and output-only work.

terminal_value.justification: Selected inspection/appraisal work can retain accountable field value, but the report-production layer and included weather/analytical services face AI commoditization; personal-property credentials are not a universal legal barrier.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No proxy was used for deterministic inputs, weights, scores, or verdict. Broad service-business multiples, general small-business owner age, and technology evidence are disclosed, low-confidence proxies for limited score inputs.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: AI savings are partly offset by fee competition where clients buy deliverables; capture therefore uses low-to-moderate retention and does not rely on credentialing as a pricing claim.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied labor share is flagged as implausibly high and blends 2024 wages with 2022 receipts; the 4-digit 5419 role mix is veterinary-dominated and does not describe 541990.
sensitivity: V changes substantially if a target-level labor share replaces 0.8164.
V changes substantially if target job mapping replaces the contaminated role mix.
B changes if the margin-based target band is replaced by selected-subsegment targets.

subsegments: Weather forecasting and analytical information services
market_boundary.selection_basis: Census confirms these are included activities. No reopened source provides revenue shares, so the dominant selection is a stated hypothesis for an investable field-and-accountability operator, not a fact about the whole code.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A changes materially, but each subsegment requires its own adoption evidence.
V is dominated by the contaminated role mix and residual assumption; target-level job mapping can reverse the result.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: Target-specific professional-service labor cost should be validated
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The supplied 0.8164 labor share mechanically amplifies V and is the most consequential data anomaly.
M is neutral by design, but purchase returns require a precise subsegment and EBITDA normalization.

reviewer_note: Every score-driving URL in S1-S7 was reopened on the access date. The packet intentionally does not treat this catch-all code as a single market or its contaminated role mix as descriptive evidence.
